text.title: mocking-strategies Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mocking-strategies as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Mocking the system under test rather than its dependencies.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If you mock the function being tested, you're not testing anything. The mock always passes regardless of whether the real implementation works.

failure-mode.description: Using Mock() instead of Mock(spec=RealClass) or autospec=True.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Plain Mock returns a Mock for any attribute access, including misspelled names. spec= or autospec=True raises AttributeError on typos, catching bugs immediately.

failure-mode.description: Over-mocking: mocking internal helpers and verifying every internal call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tests coupled to implementation details break on every refactor, even when behavior is unchanged. Verify only calls that are part of the external contract.

failure-mode.description: Not resetting mocks between tests (state leaking across tests).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Call counts and return values accumulate. Use pytest-mock's mocker fixture (auto-resets) or explicit mock.reset_mock() in setUp/tearDown.

failure-mode.description: Mocking data structures (dataclasses, Pydantic models) instead of using real instances.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mocking value objects defeats type safety and usually signals the production design needs adjustment, not the test.

failure-mode.description: Using @patch with wrong target path (patching source module, not lookup site).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: @patch('module_b.function_b') when module_a imports function_b leaves module_a's local name unaffected; the test exercises the real code.
